idea-plugin.name: MCP Hub
idea-plugin.description:
MCP server that gives AI agents access to IDE features they can't get from the command line.

Agents can read files and run terminals on their own. They can't set breakpoints, step through code, or inspect variables. MCP Hub exposes these IDE-only capabilities as MCP tools.

12 tools across 4 categories

Breakpoints (work without an active debug session)
  • list, add, update, remove
  • Conditions, log expressions, suspend control
Session management
  • List active debug sessions, stop one or all
Navigation (requires paused session)
  • Step over / into / out / continue
  • Run to a specific line
  • Every step returns a snapshot: source context, variables, call stack
Inspection (requires paused session)
  • Full debug snapshot (source + variables + stack trace)
  • Expand variables to any depth (circular references detected)
  • Switch stack frames and inspect variables at that scope
  • Evaluate PHP expressions with side effects ($bar = 'new value')

Setup

  1. Open the MCP Hub tool window (bottom panel)
  2. Click "Install .mcp.json" to configure your MCP client
  3. Start the server

Works with Claude Code, Cursor, Claude Desktop, and any other MCP client. Configurable port, optional auto-start per project.

How debugging works

You start debug sessions in PhpStorm as usual. The agent interacts with them through MCP tools: setting breakpoints, stepping through code, inspecting state. You both see the same debug session. Pair debugging with an AI.
